title: Who is rich in France?
subhead: Is a childless couple wealthy if they earn €6,438 per month after taxes? A new report seeks to address the lack of set criteria for measuring this demographic.

title: Russia exploits Ukraine's shortage of Patriots to strike with hypersonic missiles
Iran strikes Kuwait's main airport, killing one passenger, as fragile ceasefire is tested again
Trump strikes compromise over AI regulation to satisfy tech giants and MAGA base
subhead: The new executive order gives the US federal government 30 days to evaluate new AI models before they are released to the market. Trump's supporters had asked him to regulate the sector.

title: 'I am available,' investment banker Matthieu Pigasse tells the left ahead of 2027 election
subhead: The businessman, who always dreamed of a political future, held several meetings with Socialist, Green and Communist leaders, aiming to unite them all while never publicly criticizing radical leftist candidate Jean-Luc Mélenchon.

title: Sabalenka crashes out of French Open in stunning quarter-final defeat to Shnaider
subhead: The world number one led by a set and a double-break before exiting the tournament in a blaze of unforced errors, collapsing to a 3-6, 7-5, 6-0 loss in blustery conditions in Roland-Garros on Wednesday.

title: Pascal Ory, historian: Edgar Morin's 'acute sensitivity to the spirit of the times made him a trailblazer'
subhead: The member of the Académie Française pays tribute to sociologist Edgar Morin, who died on Friday at the age of 104, highlighting how Morin's life and work were driven by an enduring urge to experiment.

The 'palace' paradox: Most of France's top luxury hotels are owned by foreigners
subhead: The government's latest 'Palaces de France' list was revealed on June 2, with six new additions and four removals. The label aims to showcase this flagship sector of French expertise, even though the establishments' owners are rarely French.

title: Edgar Morin: 'I doubt humanity even as I believe in it'
subhead: Over a series of conversations held between 2024 and 2026 with Le Monde, the philosopher and WWII resistant analyzed the contemporary world's upheaval, from the rise of authoritarianism in the West to conflicts in the Middle East, as well as the impact of new technologies and environmental issues.

title: The 20 French cities where the country's wealthiest live
subhead: The city of Neuilly-sur-Seine outside Paris has the highest proportion of very wealthy households in France, according to a study by the Inequality Observatory published on Tuesday. The ranking is dominated by the western suburbs of Paris and the areas surrounding Geneva.
